titlestmt.titleproper: Edward Needles and Charles Croasdale Trump papers2869
titlestmt.author: Compiled by S. Lee

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-06-18 18:21:55 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Edward Needles and Charles Croasdale Trump papers
did.physdesc: Correspondence, Financial Records, Legal Documents, Newspapers, Photographs
did.abstract: Family papers regarding the Trump family.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Family papers include letters from Edward N. Trump to his father, Charles Newbold Trump (1881-1898); correspondence between Edward N. Trump and his son, Charles C. Trump (1898-1945); correspondence between Charles C. Trump and his sons Peter Bulley Trump and Charles Edward Trump (1942-1956), his wife Rachel Bulley Trump, and his sister Marjorie Trump; correspondence and legal documents related to the estate of Edward N. Trump (1944-1946); and correspondence and financial documents related to Edward N. Trump's management of the financial affairs of his aunt and uncle, Marie Louise and Charles A. Lasar while they were in Europe (1902-1930).

Also included are genealogical records, photographs, clippings, legal and financial documents including income tax and banking records, and student papers. Business papers concern Edward and Charles Trump's investments, developments of patents, employment by various companies including the Solvay Process Company (1885-1936), the Dolomite Marine Corporation (1934-1941), the Sanitary Metal Cap Corporation (1935-1938), Marco Company, Inc. (1940-1943), and the F.C. Haub Company (1951-1954) and includes correspondence, photographs, drawings, speeches, patents, notebooks, blueprints, and articles.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Edward Needles and Charles Croasdale Trump papers, #2869. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edward N. Trump, Cornell University Class of 1978, was an engineer with the Semet-Solvay Company, Syracuse, New York. His son, Charles C. Trump, graduated from Cornell in 1911.

unittitle.extref: Link to the paper finding aid. PDF file.

did.unittitle: I.A. Family correspondence, personal and business

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edward Needles Trump to his father, Charles Newbold Trump (1881-1898); Between E. N. Trump and his son, Charles Croasdale Trump (1898-1945); C. C. Trump and his sons, Peter Bulley and Charles Edward (Ted) Trump (1942-1956); C. C. Trump and his sister, Marjorie Trump, and his wife, Rachel Bulley Trump. Also includes letters from cousins including Helen Croasdale, and from grandmothers of C. C. Trump; also, correspondence with Lindley C. Kent, E. N. Trump's adopted son from his second marriage, to Rosamund Chambers Kent

did.unittitle: I.B. Correspondence between Edward Needles Trump and his aunt, Marie Louise Trump Lasar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: E. N. Trump acted as attorney for M. L. Lasar and her husband, Charles A. Lasar, while they were living and travelling in Europe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from William B. Cogswell to Edward N. Trump; childhood letters from friends of Charles C. Trump and John Meigs, headmaster of The Hill School (1902-1904, 1912-1914); correspondence with Carolyn Bulley Cox, sister of Rachel Bulley Trump, and John Raffles (Gunner) Cox, who were associates in the Stumpf Una-Flow Engine Company, Inc.; letters of condolence after the death of E. N. Trump

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual reports of the Humphrey Gas Pump Company and the Stumpf Una-Flow Engine Company, Inc. for 1922; notices of premiums on bonds; receipts for registered mail and a list of expenditures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Checkbook, deposit slips, and bank statements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and documents. Correspondents: C. C. Trump, Marjorie Trump, and Lindley C. Kent, co-executors of the estate, with banks, lawyers, insurance companies, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include will of Katherine Croasdale Trump; bankbook

did.unittitle: I.E.1. Mr. and Mrs. Charles A. Lasar's accounts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents related to E. N. Trump's management of the Lasars' affairs

scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous notes, photos, and information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs, Harvard Class Day passes, correspondence, a composition, a song sheet, a receipt, "Sons of '09" member receipt; a eulogy of a former headmaster of The Hill School

did.unittitle: Cornell University Class of 1878 "Thirtieth Year History"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material documenting C. C. Trump's interest in developing an aerodrome

did.unittitle: Lehigh Airport Competition announcement and program
Report with supplements: Location of Airport, Philadelphia, PA
Regional Planning Federation, the Philadelphia Tri-State District: picture of the region
Meeting of Society of Automotive Engineers, Philadelphia Section
Terminal Aerodrome for West Philadelphia, PA, designed by Charles C. Trump

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented at meeting of the Society of Automotive Engineers, Philadelphia Section, May 8, 1929

did.unittitle: I.J. Clippings, Biographical material, and Ephemera
List of minerals in the collection of Dr. David T. Day, purchased December 1889
Notes, maps, advertisements; essay on child health, flyers, name tags

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mr. and Mrs. Charles C. (Rachel Bulley) Trump

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks, correspondence, photographs, and patent documents from the businesses in which family members invested or were employed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: E. N. Trump was hired by William B. Cogswell in 1882 as first engineer to construct the first U.S. plant for the manufacture of soda. He became general manager and chief engineer in charge of operations upon its completion. He was later promoted to vice-president, director, and chairman of the Technical Committee. As a construction engineer he built several plants for Solvay By-Product Coke Ovens. He left the firm in 1930. This series includes correspondence, 1885-1936, organizational chart, 1907, clippings, programs, publications, speeches, and articles by Trump.

did.unittitle: Letters from Frederick Rowland Hazard and William B. Cogswell
The Solvay Process Company, The Solvay Process Alkali
By-Laws of the Solvay Process Company
Solvay Products, Various forms and uses
Discours prononce par M Ernest Solvay
Cinquantieme Anniversaire de la Fondation de la Societe Solvay et Cie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Banqiete, fete, seating lists

did.unittitle: Discours prononce au nom du personnel des usines du procede Solvay a l'occasion des 25 annees de direction de MM Ernest et Alfred Solvay
Clippings, business cards, employment contract for E. N. Trump
Commemoration of the Twenty-first Anniversary of the Solvay Process Company
Speeches and articles by E. N. Trump
Documents pertaining to E. N. Trump's investments in this ship-building company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, correspondence, and a brochure

did.unittitle: II.C. F. C. Haub Company, Inc., Philadelphia
Documents pertaining to C. C. Trump's position as consulting and mechanical engineer and a sales engineer for this company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and work sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In 1937, E. N. Trump was the treasurer and consulting engineer for the Sanitary Metal Cap Corporation, while C.C. Trump was the director from 1931-1938. E .N. Trump owned $350,000 worth of preferred stock in the company. The company filed for bankruptcy in 1938, but in 1954-1955, C. C. Trump and William D. Slattery, former president of the company. and the Trumps' lawyer, sold the remaining equipment and closed the account. See also Subseries I.C., correspondence with Carolyn Cox and J. R. Cox.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: C.C. Trump was interested in developing a patent which he held for "Drying in Upward suspension of gases." Towards this end, a non-exclusive license agreement was signed between the Trumps and the Marco Company, Inc. in January 1942

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: C. C. Trump used these files to complete his father's unfinished business after his death in 1944, and removed some papers to the other two files

did.unittitle: J, including Asmus Jabs - E. N. Trump correspondence; Major Cuthbert Johnson
T, U, V (Trump correspondence transferred to family correspondence)
II.F.2. C. C. Trump, E. N. Trump Subject file

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing Agreement and Declaration of Trust

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and documents from the Savings Institution of Sandy Spring, Maryland, the Western National Bank of Baltimore, and the Syracuse Merchants National Bank and Trust Company

scopecontent.p: [Note: patents are in II.G.]
